title: Error when using special characters in credentials
author: dirk-schuermann
body_snippet: As the risk of being hacked increases, passwords should be improved. Especially through the use of special characters. Microsoft even recommends using Alt+0xxx codes. Well, there's a problem I've lost my teeth on. After configuration of access data in the tree (inheritance off), Windows asks for an error message about incorrect access data before each connection is established again for the name and password. The password entered there is accepted. The same password is also stored in the tree un...

iterations.description: Issue body is empty and comments indicate it may be stale/already addressed; needs confirmation of reproducibility on current fork version.
Wontfix: Special characters in credentials are handled correctly in current XML/SQL serialization — needs reproduction steps

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet:
Looks like an Encoding issue with the configuration file that needs to be solved. There might be similar issues here, didn't have time to look for them. 

Regarding the recommendation to use Alt-Codes I'd like to get this source, it's hard for me to imagine Microsoft recommends to use characters that cannot be entered on all devices...

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: At curent stage its will be change soon, the tree will not store any sensitive information (only id and source from where fetch once needed)

notes: Stale: special characters in credentials. Reporter never confirmed on latest build.
